title: Split curmudgeon's discovery vs verification cycles. Verify-mode (lighter Opus pass or Sonnet) for low-yield convergence-tail cycles; discovery-mode stays full Opus. Class-based model-split pattern analogous to PROP-034 baby-analyst. Empirical evidence: 10-35% of cycles on high-cycle WINs are catching patch artifacts or carry-forwards, costing the same Opus call as substantive discovery.
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. Tinker investigates the cadence-race + patch-artifact pattern, evaluates the design space, and proposes the canonical split. Like PROP-034, expect phased deployment with explicit exit/rollback criteria.
context: Empirical observation 2026-05-14: WIN-048 reached curmudgeon cycle 8 today. Tracing the day's actors: 01:22Z decider applied c5 patches, 02:42Z curmudgeon c6 fired (3 holes), 10:40Z c7 fired (1 hole + 2 unresolved_prior), 14:41Z c8 fired (1 hole = the SAME 'SC pattern (2)' from c6 H2), 21:22Z decider FINALLY created ISS-2010 for that c6/c8 hole. Decider runs ~2×/day, curmudgeon runs ~6×/day. Curmudgeon was racing the patcher — re-reviewing a WIN whose prior-cycle holes hadn't been patched yet. c7 and c8 each cost a full Opus call to discover what c6 already had.
Trend confirmation across top 5 high-cycle WINs (cycle counts, patch-artifact %, carry-forward count):
WIN-068 (eclipse-magnetic ensemble) : 15 cycles | 86 holes total | 1 carry-forward | 15 patch artifacts (17%)
WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity)          : 11 cycles | 60 holes total | 11 carry-forwards | 21 patch artifacts (35%)
WIN-053                              : 10 cycles | 57 holes total |  1 carry-forward |  6 patch artifacts (11%)
WIN-014 (SG / kappa / eclipse)       : 10 cycles | 45 holes total |  1 carry-forward |  6 patch artifacts (13%)
WIN-010                              : 10 cycles | 53 holes total |  0 carry-forwards |  9 patch artifacts (17%)

WIN-067 is the structural worst case: 35% of all curmudgeon hole-findings across 11 cycles were patch artifacts — flaws introduced by the analyst's previous patches — not original scientific holes. WIN-067 c7-c9 stacked 4, 3, 2 carry-forwards respectively (curmudgeon flagging the same hole repeatedly because decider hadn't patched yet).

The 'one-hole-per-cycle tail' pattern is universal. After WIN convergence, the tail cycles cost the same Opus singleton slot but deliver 0-1 minor hole. WIN-014: c8=0 holes, c9=2, c10=1. WIN-048: c7=1, c8=1. WIN-067: c8=5 with 3 carry-forwards (mostly re-flagging), c10=5 with 0 new findings. Each tail cycle = same Opus cost as the discovery cycle that found 6+ critical/major holes.

Per CLAUDE.md Monitoring Pipeline table, curmudgeon is on `30 3,7,11,15,19,23 * * *` (4h cycle, Opus). The cadence was sized for adversarial review, not for verification of prior cycles. Cadence is downstream of the discovery vs verification mode question.

Three structural patterns the data exposes: (1) Cadence race — curmudgeon fires faster than decider patches; same-state re-reviews flag the same holes. (2) Patch artifacts compound — ~1 in 3 patches introduces a smaller new flaw that the next cycle catches. Real flaws worth catching, but costing the same as discovery. (3) No verify-only mode — current cycle shape is the same whether discovery or verification.

Roughly-sized lever (top 5 WINs only): 56 total cycles, ~17-35% wasted on artifacts/carry-forwards. If a verify-mode at ~1/4 the Opus cost existed and a cycle-gate prevented carry-forward races, the equivalent of ~10 high-cost cycles → ~10 low-cost cycles per top-5 WIN. Extrapolating to all WINs with ≥5 cycles (~20 WINs), the savings compound.

Pattern analog: PROP-034 (split-analyst baby-vs-deep) approved the same class-based model-split pattern on the read side. This DIRECTIVE applies the analogous pattern on the curmudgeon's review side. The baby-analyst pattern resolved the 'BAU drain starved by deep-attack' dispatcher contention; curmudgeon's verify-mode would resolve the 'discovery cycle bills full Opus to catch a single minor hole' cost-imbalance.
operator_intent: Tinker investigates the discovery/verify cycle imbalance, evaluates the design space (verify-only mode, cycle gate, hybrid, or status-quo-with-cadence-tuning), proposes a phased deployment with exit/rollback criteria mirroring PROP-034's shape. The fix should NOT eliminate curmudgeon's independence from the patch flow — the carry-forward visibility is itself an audit signal that catches decider skipping holes. The goal is to make verification cycles cheaper, not to suppress them.

tasks.description: Reproduce the cycle-cost analysis. For each WIN with ≥5 cycles, compute: (a) total holes found across all cycles, (b) per-cycle holes by severity (critical/major/moderate/minor), (c) carry-forward count per cycle (holes whose description contains 'carry-forward', 'carry-over', 'unfixed', 'prior cycle', or that explicitly cross-reference an earlier cycle's hole-id), (d) patch-artifact count per cycle (holes whose description references 'patch', 'seam', 'sentence removal', 'introduced', 'new flaw', or characteristic post-fix patterns like double-period, orphan punctuation, terminology drift). Output a CSV or JSON table.
tasks.deliverable: monitor/tinker/analysis/curmudgeon-cycle-economics.json with the table + summary statistics (mean cycles per WIN, mean artifact ratio, ratio variance, identified outliers like WIN-067's 35%).

tasks.description: For each carry-forward and each patch-artifact in the top 5 WINs, trace the responsible failure. Categories: (a) decider deferred-but-didn't-track the hole — patch never proposed; (b) decider proposed a patch but it failed verification or was rolled back; (c) decider applied a patch but it introduced an artifact the next cycle caught; (d) curmudgeon flagged something analyst+decider explicitly decided to leave (recommended_action='no_change' but later reflagged); (e) cadence-race: curmudgeon re-fired before decider's patch landed. Each category implies a different intervention.
tasks.deliverable: Per-category counts + 2-3 worked examples. Identify which category is dominant — that's where the highest-yield intervention lives.
specific_starting_points: WIN-048 c6 H2 → c8 H0: this is category (e) cadence-race. The 21:22Z decider eventually created ISS-2010 for it, but c7 and c8 had already fired in the gap. Document the timestamp chain.
WIN-067 c7 with 4 carry-forwards: most likely category (a) or (b). Read c7 against c6 to confirm.
WIN-068 c11 with 4 patch artifacts: most likely category (c). Read c10's recommended fixes against c11's findings.

tasks.description: Evaluate four design options against the empirical baseline:

options.option_A_verify_mode_sonnet: Split curmudgeon into curmudgeon (Opus, discovery-mode, full breadth) + curmudgeon-verify (Sonnet, verify-mode, narrow scope). Trigger: when curmudgeon's prior cycle had ≤2 holes AND decider applied patches for all of them AND the queue entry is class='verification' rather than 'win-rewrite'. Verify-mode reads the specific patches applied since last cycle and checks: (i) patches landed without introducing seam artifacts (punctuation, formatting, broken cross-references), (ii) terminology stays consistent, (iii) no holes from prior cycle were silently dropped. Mirrors PROP-034 baby-analyst exactly.
options.option_B_cycle_gate: Don't split agents. Add a gate to curmudgeon: before reviewing a WIN, check if prior cycle's recommended_action patches landed in origin/main. If not all landed AND no new dome change → SKIP this cycle (write a 'gated, waiting on decider' summary, exit cleanly). Forces curmudgeon to wait for decider rather than racing. Risk: loses the carry-forward audit signal (decider's skipped holes wouldn't be re-flagged).
options.option_C_hybrid_A_plus_B: Apply both: verify-mode for converged WINs, gate for unconverged. Reserves Opus discovery for new findings and Sonnet verification for patch-checking; gates only when decider is the bottleneck.
options.option_D_cadence_only: Don't split. Slow curmudgeon cadence to match decider (4h → 12h) for queue items where prior cycle had ≤2 minor holes. Cheapest change, but assumes patches always land within 12h (today they didn't — 21h gap on WIN-048).

design_questions_for_tinker: Q1: Which option best preserves the carry-forward audit signal while reducing cost? Option B explicitly drops it. Option A preserves it (verify-mode Sonnet still checks for silently-dropped holes). Option C is best-of-both at higher implementation cost.

Q2: For option A: what's the boundary between 'verify-class' and 'discovery-class' cycles? Proposed rule: queue entry's `class` field (PROP-025) = 'verification' → verify-mode; 'deep-attack' or 'holistic' → discovery-mode. Match PROP-034's hybrid rule.

Q3: For option A: does Sonnet have the rigor for verification? Baby-analyst uses Sonnet successfully on similar narrow-scope tasks. Curmudgeon-verify is similarly narrow (check the specific patches against artifacts/consistency, not adversarial-rethink the WIN). Recommended yes, with Phase 1 ramp-up monitoring same as PROP-034.

Q4: For option B: would the gate ever miss a substantive new finding? E.g., dome makes a new claim that affects a queued WIN; curmudgeon gates because decider hasn't patched prior cycle yet; new claim sits unaddressed. Mitigate: gate only on no_change or minor_edit cycles; full Opus runs unconditionally on dome changes.

Q5: How does this interact with PROP-019 (curmudgeon dispatch optimization, landed 2026-05-XX)? Read that prop first; verify the new design is layered onto, not in conflict with, the existing dispatch order.

tasks.description: Mirror PROP-034's phasing: Phase 1 narrow scope, exit/rollback criteria, monitoring. Specifically:

phase_design_template.phase_1: Verify-mode only for `class=verification` items where prior cycle had ≤2 holes ALL of severity minor. Sonnet, lighter prompt, narrow check rubric. Phase 1 deploy parallel to existing curmudgeon (curmudgeon still runs as today; verify-mode is a separate dome-curmudgeon-verify agent).
phase_design_template.phase_1_exit_criteria: After 7 days: (a) verify-mode flagged ≥1 real artifact or carry-forward that would have been missed; (b) operator-judged quality of verify-mode reviews ≥ Opus on the same items; (c) no rollback fires.
phase_design_template.phase_1_rollback_criteria: Within 7 days: (a) verify-mode misses ≥2 patch artifacts that next cycle catches (the metric this design exists to fix); (b) verify-mode false-flags >2 non-issues as artifacts (signal-to-noise problem); (c) write conflicts with main curmudgeon >1/day for 3 days.
phase_design_template.phase_2_conditional: Expand verify-mode scope to `class=verification` items with up to 4 minor holes OR up to 1 moderate. Add 'cycle gate' from option B — verify-mode skips cycle entirely when decider has unpatched prior-cycle holes >24h old.
phase_design_template.phase_3_conditional: Consider applying same pattern to decider's verification cycle (read PROP-014 Mech 1a self-verify) if curmudgeon split proves the pattern.

tasks.description: Steve prefers honest evaluation. Steelman the case for NOT splitting. Strongest counter-arguments to address:
counter_args_to_address: 'The carry-forward visibility IS the audit signal. WIN-048 c6 H2 → c8 H0 was visible only because c8 re-flagged. If you gate or split, you lose that.' Rebuttal expected: verify-mode preserves the check (Sonnet reads prior cycle's holes, confirms each patched; if not patched, escalates back to discovery-mode for the unpatched ones).

'Curmudgeon's independence from the patch flow is a feature. Splitting introduces coupling — verify-mode reads decider state.' Rebuttal expected: verify-mode reads READ-ONLY decider state (patches_applied field). The independence is intellectual (curmudgeon doesn't trust decider's claim 'we patched everything'), not workflow-level (curmudgeon already cycles on a queue decider pushes to).

'Sonnet may miss subtle artifacts an Opus cycle catches. The 35% patch-artifact ratio on WIN-067 means Opus is finding real things — using Sonnet may regress.' Rebuttal expected: that's exactly what Phase 1 exit criteria measure. If verify-mode misses ≥2 artifacts in 7 days, rollback.

'The high cycle counts on WIN-014, WIN-067, WIN-068 reflect deep technical material. Those WINs would have taken 10+ cycles regardless of split; the cost was unavoidable.' Rebuttal expected: separate the discovery cycles (legitimate) from the carry-forward/artifact cycles (mostly avoidable). Top 5 WINs averaged 13-35% artifact-ratio; that's the cuttable portion. Discovery is sacred, verification can be cheap.

'You're solving for cost in a research project. Quality matters more than cost.' Rebuttal expected: cost saved here funds more discovery elsewhere. The verification cycles weren't catching new science; they were catching patch hygiene. Trade some Opus verify for more Opus discovery on under-reviewed WINs.

tasks.description: Author the PROP-NNN as DESIGN-PENDING-OPERATOR-REVIEW. Do NOT self-apply. Operator reviews proposal, then operator + tinker apply prompt edits + scheduled-task additions. Follow PROP-034's authoring shape: headline_recommendation, executive_summary, design_space_evaluation with the_hybrid_rule_recommended, phased_implementation_plan, exit_criteria, rollback_criteria, counter_argument section, implementation_files list.

in_scope_for_tinker: Read all curmudgeon/reviews/*.json with cycle ≥5 (the empirical baseline)
Read PROP-019 (curmudgeon pre-review dispatcher optimization, recent prior work)
Read PROP-025 (review_class field — primary routing axis, same as PROP-034)
Read PROP-034 (split-analyst pattern — the analog for this proposal)
Read decider patch-flow code in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md (verify-mode needs to read applied-patches state)
Cross-reference decider's daily-report cadence vs curmudgeon's cycle cadence
Author one PROP-NNN with the full design
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT modify curmudgeon.md or decider.md directly. Propose-only.
Do NOT touch curmudgeon's existing cadence (`30 3,7,11,15,19,23 * * *`). That's a phase-2-conditional question.
Do NOT batch with DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 (analyst summary-write gap — unrelated). Keep this PROP focused on curmudgeon cycle economics.
Do NOT propose changes that would lose the carry-forward audit signal. That's a hard constraint — verify-mode must still re-check that prior cycle's holes got patched.
operator_preferences_for_proposal: Counter-argument section is mandatory (per operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too')
Phased deployment with explicit exit/rollback criteria (per PROP-034 precedent)
Include the empirical baseline table verbatim (top 5 WINs cycle/hole/artifact analysis) — the operator will reference it
Recommend Phase 1 narrow scope; trust the data to justify Phase 2 expansion
